reviews.text:
I have not retained McDougal, Boehmer, Foley, Lyon, Mitchell & Erickson, but I have dealt with this firm extensively in an adversarial capacity while they represented the City of El Cajon in code-enforcement litigation involving my property.
Based on my direct experience, I found the firm’s litigation approach to be aggressive, inefficient, and poorly managed. Despite reportedly billing the City millions of dollars in legal fees, their presentation at trial was disorganized and unpersuasive. The Court ultimately saw through the City’s position and allowed my cross-complaint to proceed, including causes of action for federal civil-rights violations under 42 U.S.C. § 1983, due process violations, and equal protection / selective enforcement.
In my opinion, the firm’s handling of the matter reflected a lack of professionalism and strategic clarity. Work appeared to be delegated to outside or secondary attorneys, which only compounded the inefficiencies and confusion in court.
This review reflects my personal experience and opinion as a litigant opposing this firm—not as a client. Anyone considering hiring this firm, particularly public agencies spending taxpayer funds, should conduct careful due diligence and independently evaluate whether the value delivered aligns with the fees incurred.

reviews.text:
Mr. Kern came to me highly recommended, By an by a prestigious attorney in San Diego, and now I understand why. Even in what was an emotionally charged and frankly petty case, he remained grounded—focused on the law and the facts. He never allowed the lowbrow tactics of the opposing party to derail the process or distract the judge from what truly mattered: the truth.

Navigating the court system is tough, especially when your opponent is a skilled manipulator—an expert-level narcissist who could force out tears on cue. I genuinely feel for judges; they don’t know the people standing in front of them, and it’s easy to see how one could get swept up in the emotional drama. At one point, it felt like the judge was leaning into the gaslighting and theatrics of the other side.

But Mr. Kern calmly and firmly brought the courtroom back to reality. He reminded the court of the law and refocused attention on the lack of real evidence, the doctored documents, and the fact that the parties had no interaction whatsoever. This case should never have made it to court—it was a bullying attempt filled with baseless claims and manipulative behavior. Yet it nearly succeeded, simply due to emotional manipulation.

Thankfully, Mr. Kern never wavered. He remained composed, repeated the facts, and skillfully countered the narcissist’s tactics with professionalism and clarity. He ensured that the law—not the performance—prevailed. I truly couldn’t have done it without him.

Even in what seems like a cut-and-dry case, you never know which way things will go. Judges are human, and abusers can be incredibly convincing. That’s why I strongly recommend having an attorney—and I highly recommend Mr. Kern. He didn’t let the bully win.

I retained Ms. Brown's services in December 2025 to represent me in a Temporary Restraining Order case. After explaining my situation during our initial consultation, she said the three words anyone falsely accused of serious allegations desperately hopes to hear from an attorney: "I believe you." The relief I felt in that moment was immeasurable.
Over the next two months, Ms. Brown worked professionally, diligently, and relentlessly on my case. She successfully had the stay-away order reduced from 300 feet to just 15 feet, which allowed me to continue working as the maintenance person at the apartment complex where I lived prior to being suddenly removed on November 21, 2025. She also ensured the court admonished the petitioner not to remove anything from the apartment while the case was being contested.
We went to trial on January 27, 2026, and that is when I truly saw Ms. Brown in her element. She is an absolute professional and master of her craft. She was relentless in her questioning of my accuser, repeatedly objecting to improper or evasive answers. Objections the judge sustained time and time again. To put it plainly, Ms. Brown was a pit bull.
When it was my turn to testify, Ms. Brown guided me through the process with precision and care. Her questioning was so well-orchestrated that I felt calm, confident, and protected. I knew exactly when to answer "yes" or "no" and when to expand, which made all the difference. She was simply brilliant.
At the end of testimony, the judge decided not to allow the TRO to move forward. We won!
In closing, I can say without hesitation that I chose the exact right attorney to represent me.

If you are facing a Temporary or Domestic Violence restraining order and want to protect your reputation, your future, and your good name, hire Ms. Brown.
She gave me my life, and my name back.

reviews.text:
My wife, previously in contracts, sent my lawyer at this firm a document that we just wanted her to "make official" having to do with my ex-wife.  She returned it with many grammatical and factual errors that demonstrated she was giving very little care to our issue.

We were then taken to an evidentiary hearing by my ex-wife.  Both before and during the hearing, I mentioned over and over that I wanted an app (Talking Parents) to talk with my ex-wife so she didn't feel free to constantly harass me.  I hoped that the slightest feeling that her messages were being looked at would keep her from lashing out at me constantly.  My lawyer told me that she had gotten the agreement for the app already even when I mentioned it during the hearing she did not bring it up. Surprise, surprise, there was no agreement on the app.

We also made a plan to ask my ex-wife to pay for our lawyer fees since taking me to an evidentiary hearing that was obviously not needed.  She did not bring that up either.

When my lawyer was cross-examining my ex wife she asked maybe four questions, then turned to me and asked, "want me to ask anything else?"  I was completely unprepared for this.  The whole situation was overwhelming to me and I was struggling with emotions at being in this situation.

After that, my ex-wife's lawyers mentioned there were typos in the agreement.  As I mentioned, I was very overwhelmed and struggling with frustrations and I wasn't listening to a list of typos.  During this process the opposing counsel changed some very important lines about holiday dates and she did not mentioned to me that that had happened.  I do take fault for this because she did ask if I agreed to the typos being changed and I had said I did because I only heard about a comma here or a miss-spelling there.  She could clearly see I was struggling and I do wish she had reiterated what had changed.  Again, I share some fault for this issue.

Finally, the agreement at Family Court Services included a line that said roughly, "if the parents do not come to an agreement on a halfway dropoff location, (x) parking lot will be the default dropoff place."  I am a very fair minded person, reading that to me says that the parents have to make an attempt at coming to an agreement before that default is used.  I moved after the FCS agreement but I didn't think twice about that line because I knew I was flexible on the halfway spot.  My ex-wife has since completely not agreed to even discuss the halfway meeting point and I am now driving 30 minutes from my house while she drives 10 to the "default dropoff spot."  My lawyer should have known this was a legally tenuous line and had it changed.

After the hearing, my lawyer sent me an email telling me she tried to hand me off to another lawyer but that other lawyer didn't want to go up against my ex-wife's lawyer.  I have to say, my ex-wife is a very angry person, but she found an equally angry lawyer that this entire firm is afraid to go up against.  If that is the type of lawyer you want, I recommend hiring Kathy Minella.  She will charge you $700 an hour but apparently she makes other lawyers shake in their boots.

Also, after the hearing I brought up my issues with my lawyer and she sent me a legal response PDF where she blames me for the issues I mentioned above and also told me that I don't really understand what the app I wanted was anyways.  It was a completely CYA response in the form of a very legal document that demonstrated she was doing more lawyering against me, her client, than my ex-wife.

This was absolutely awful experience and I am upset not only with my lawyer but also Bud who told me she was an "excellent litigator."  This couldn't have been further from the truth.

Bottom line: If you aren't prepared to do more than half of the lawyering yourself, avoid this law firm.  If you just need to prepare for FCS, Bud is great at that.
